findingDetails.title: A scoped Rust reference bypasses the mandatory OVERVIEW wrapper
findingDetails.evidence: After the H1 intro and separator, the file starts with unnumbered Error Style content and contains no OVERVIEW/Purpose/When-to-Use wrapper required by R3.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Deterministic structure scan covered all 163 configured files and isolated this one wrapper omission; direct reread confirmed lines 13-21.
findingDetails.claim: One in-scope reference omits the mandatory OVERVIEW/Purpose/When-to-Use wrapper while the packet claims every scoped file has it.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Forced reference-type validation across all 163 files returned zero validator issues; the file and validator no-H2 path were reread.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The file could be intentionally exempt or outside the historical transformation manifest, but the rendered scope includes its root and no exception is documented.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Approved scope amendment or authoritative manifest proving this file exempt from R3.

findingDetails.title: The CSRF checklist labels a JavaScript-accessible session cookie as good guidance
findingDetails.evidence: The GOOD example creates a session cookie with document.cookie, which cannot set HttpOnly; lines 247-253 later prescribe server-side HttpOnly and call JavaScript-accessible session cookies bad.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The scope-corrected 157-Markdown search isolated this as the only positively labelled JavaScript session-cookie assignment; direct counterevidence in the same file confirms the server-side HttpOnly rule.
findingDetails.claim: The security checklist positively labels a JavaScript-set session cookie even though JavaScript cannot set HttpOnly and the same checklist later forbids JavaScript-accessible session cookies.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: The complete cookie guidance was reread; SameSite=Strict and Secure mitigate CSRF/transport risks, but the later HttpOnly rule confirms the JavaScript session-cookie example is incomplete and contradictory.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The first snippet may only demonstrate SameSite rather than complete session-cookie creation, but the GOOD label and session cookie name do not communicate that limitation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the snippet is explicitly limited to a non-sensitive demonstration cookie and no longer labels JavaScript session-cookie creation as production-safe.

findingDetails.title: Production CDN-loader guidance omits integrity enforcement and accepts any caller-provided URL
findingDetails.evidence: The production-tested helper assigns a caller-provided URL to script.src and treats version pinning plus error handling as sufficient, without an origin allowlist or integrity attribute.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact searches across all 157 in-scope Markdown files found dynamic script.src examples in five references, one optional crossOrigin assignment, and no integrity assignment; the representative guide was directly reread.

spec_code.note: carried forward from iteration 1; saturated correctness sweep not repeated

checklist_evidence.note: carried forward; BLOCKED approach not retried

noveltyJustification: A security-only pivot found one contradictory session-cookie trust-boundary defect and one third-party script hardening gap; it did not repeat the saturated correctness structural sweep.
edgeCases: Explicit **/*.md globs match 157 files while the configured 163 count includes six non-Markdown assets; excluded hits produced no finding.
SameSite/Secure are valid mitigations, but do not make a JavaScript-accessible session cookie HttpOnly.
Memory trigger timed out; direct evidence fallback used.
ruledOut: approved direct execution of user input
